name: Automotive
cardText: Warn drivers and ADAS stacks about pedestrians, cyclists and riders concealed behind vehicles and structures.

meta.title: Automotive
meta.description: Viziblezone for automotive: alert drivers and ADAS systems about vulnerable road users hidden behind obstacles, before line of sight.

hero.title:
Perception beyond
the sensor suite
hero.lede: Cameras, radar and lidar end where line of sight ends. Viziblezone extends the vehicle's awareness to the pedestrian behind the parked van and the cyclist past the blind corner.

scenario.title:
The dart-out
problem
paragraphs: A child steps out from between parked cars. A delivery rider crosses behind a truck. In these scenarios the driver and the vehicle's own sensors see the person only when they emerge — leaving a reaction window that is often shorter than braking distance.

Viziblezone closes that gap. The pedestrian's phone announces presence and trajectory directly to the approaching vehicle, which raises a targeted alert seconds before visual contact. The driver slows down before the situation becomes an emergency.

items.title: Earlier warnings
items.text: Alerts arrive before line of sight, adding seconds where onboard sensors add fractions of a second.

items.title: No blind spots
items.text: Occlusions, darkness, glare and weather degrade optical sensors. Direct device-to-device awareness is indifferent to all of them.

items.title: ADAS & HMI ready
items.text: Risk events are delivered as clean, prioritized inputs to ADAS logic, instrument cluster or infotainment.

items.title: Software, not hardware
items.text: No additional sensors on the vehicle and no roadside units. Value scales with the installed base of protected phones.

deployment.title:
How it lands
in a vehicle
paragraphs: Integration targets the vehicle's existing software stack: alerts can surface through the infotainment platform, the ADAS domain, or a fleet application. On the pedestrian side, protection ships as an SDK inside partner apps or natively in the device.
bullets: In-vehicle: infotainment app, ADAS input, or fleet telematics integration
Pedestrian side: SDK embedded in partner apps or native device integration
Pilot format: instrumented vehicles in a defined urban zone with measurable KPIs

cta.title:
Put it in
a vehicle
cta.lede: We work with OEMs, Tier-1s and fleets on integrations and pilots.
